chemistry.mechanism_type: Outer sphere electron transfer from photoabsorber to co-catalyst

mechanistic_details.key_intermediate: Reduced Ni(I) center and COF radical anion

chemistry.scope_summary: Single photocatalytic system using TpDTz COF with NiME co-catalyst for sustained H2 evolution from water over 70 hours with maximum rate of 941 μmol h⁻¹ g⁻¹ and TON > 103

optimization_table.key_findings: TpDTz-NiME system achieved 941 μmol H2 evolution in 1 h with TONNi > 103

figures.caption: Figure 1. Figure 2. Structural characterization of TpDTz COF.  a)   13 C and  15 N CP–MAS solid-state NMR spectrum of  TpDTz  COF. Calculated  NMR chemical shifts for the TpDTz-NMR model (Figure S49) obtained on B97-2/pcS-2//PBE0-D3/def2-TZVP level of theory (Table  S4 & S5) are shown as grey dashes; b) Argon adsorption-desorption isotherm for  TpDTz  COF recorded at 87 K; inset: calculated pore  size distr

figures.flow_path: Mass flow controller → Photocatalytic Jacketed Reactor → Pressure Controller → Auto sampler → GC Oven → BID/MS → Atmosphere
figures.key_information: Photocatalytic reactor system with solar irradiation through quartz window, temperature control via thermostat, pressure control, and inline GC-MS analysis
figures.caption: Figure 3. Continuous flow photocatalytic reactor design . Schematic diagram of the designed continuous flow photocatalytic re- actor system (red streamlines are the continuous flow pathway of gas). In contrast, the batch configuration involves mass flow con- trollers as dead-ends after back purging the initial headspace, and replaces the autosampler by a septa-port or a manual sampling  valve.

Figure 4. Photocatalytic H 2  evolution.  a)   A comparison of photocatalytic H 2  evolution rates in water (H 2 O) and deuterium oxide  (D 2 O), using  TpDTz  COF over 72 h and  EB  dye under AM 1.5 light irradiation [COF photosensitizer: 5 mg of  TpDTz  COF in 10 ml of  H 2 O/D 2 O with 10 vol.% TEoA, 0.5 mg of Ni(OAc) 2  and 1.4  μ L of ME at a final pH of 8.5; dye photosensitizer: 1.33 mg of

Figure 5. Reaction limitations insights . a) A general schematic of the proposed pathway for H 2  evolution (color code: grey; C, red;  O, yellow; S, blue; N and light pink/white; H); b) proposed key steps of the photocatalytic H 2  evolution reaction with  TpDTz  COF and  NiME cluster co-catalyst. figure_descriptions: Reactor scheme showing continuous flow photocatalytic reactor system with solar irradiation through quartz window, temperature control via thermostat, pressure control, and inline GC-MS analysis
Mechanistic schemes showing proposed pathway for H2 evolution and key steps of photocatalytic process

conflicts: Pass3 indicated back_pressure_bar=null but Figure 3 reactor scheme clearly shows a Pressure Controller (BPR), corrected to true based on figure
notes: This is a photocatalytic hydrogen evolution study with both batch and flow configurations. The reactor scheme figure provides key information about the flow setup including BPR presence. Main limitation is lack of optimization table data and incomplete flow parameters.
